violations.description: Preventing contamination from equipment, utensils, and wiping cloths. — --Cloth for wiping food spills was not stored in sanitizing solution (at proper concentration) between uses. -Sani bucket found with less than 150ppm Quat solution.

Refrigerated Storage. — --Refrigerator or cold storage was insufficient or ineffective at maintaining potentially hazardous food at a temperature of 41F or less. -Top of left table found with Boba temperature of 47F. -Jam at top of right table (set on top of lid due to ice in well) found at 54F.

Cleaning frequency of equipment and utensils. — --Nonfood contact surface was not cleaned as frequently as required. -Residue/debris at pot lid and shelf liner at tapioca prep shelf in back room. -Residue front of hand soap dispenser of backroom hand sink.
Use of poisonous and toxic materials. — --Poisonous or toxic material was not used and/or applied according to manufacturer's label. -Sanitizer spray with solution greater than 400ppm Quat.
Preventing contamination by employees. — --Employee * observed handling ready-to-eat food * with bare hands.

Preventing contamination from equipment, utensils, and wiping cloths. — --Cloth for wiping food spills was not stored in sanitizing solution (at proper concentration) between uses. -Sanitizer bucket and spray both found at 500ppm Quat. *Should be 200ppm, (made to sanitizer instruction of 1oz per gallon of water).--In-use utensil was not properly stored during pauses in food preparation or dispensing. -Multiple scoops laying with handles in product (drink powders).

Preventing contamination by consumers. — --Utensil was not suitable or dispensed effectively for self-service operations of ready-to-eat foods. -Single use cup used as scoop left in bulk coffee powder and bulk sugar.

Cleaning frequency of equipment and utensils. — --Food contact surface used with potentially hazardous food was not cleaned every four hours while in use or as frequently as required. -Utensils at coffee station are fully washed (wash, rinsed, and sanitized) daily, but only washed and rinsed at prep sinks every 2 hours or less. *MUST have full wash rinse and sanitize every 4 hours or less.--Nonfood contact surface was not cleaned as frequently as required. -Sticky residue/debris at handles of refrigeration and drawers throughout prep area. -Debris/residue at lids/exteriors of bulk containers used for powder creamer, sugar and salt. -Residue at edges/basins of 3 comp sink-due for a deep clean.

Single-service and single-use articles. — --Single-service item was not stored in a proper location to be protected from contamination. -2 cases of product on floor in front of 3 comp sink.--Single-service item was not stored, handled, dispensed in a manner to protect from contamination. -Spoons in drawer are not "faced" same direction to protect food contact surfaces.
Proper storage, no-reuse (1 pt deducted)
Clean, employee dressing area, cleaning equipment storage, living/sleeping quarters separate (1 pt deducted)
Proper storage, labeling, use (3 pts deducted)
Available, adequate capacity, able to maintain proper temperature (4 pts deducted)
Date marking accomplished, discarded (2 pts deducted)
Cleaning frequency, maintained clean (2 pts deducted)
Cleaning frequency, maintained clean (1 pt deducted)
Proper storage, no-reuse (1 pt deducted)
Number, location, accessibility, soap and paper towels provided (3 pts deducted)
Storage, properly handled (1 pt deducted)
Clean, proper use, storage, sanitizing solution (2 pts deducted)
Available, adequate capacity, able to maintain proper temperature (4 pts deducted)
